output.description: Capture and log stdout/stderr of the signing command. Accepts bool or template string (e.g., "{{ .IsSnapshot }}").

stdin.description: Content written to the signing command's stdin.

stdin_file.description: Path to a file whose content is written to the signing command's stdin.

attachments.description: Slack legacy attachments (typed for schema validation).

blocks.description: Slack Block Kit blocks (typed for schema validation).

enabled.description: Enable Slack announcements (supports template expressions).

icon_emoji.description: Override the webhook's default icon with an emoji (e.g. ":rocket:").

icon_url.description: Override the webhook's default icon with an image URL.

message_template.description: Message template for the Slack post. Default: "{{ .ProjectName }} {{ .Tag }} is out! Check it out at {{ .ReleaseURL }}"

webhook_url.description: Slack incoming webhook URL. Use template `{{ Env.SLACK_WEBHOOK }}` to reference an environment variable.

SlackAttachment.description: A Slack legacy attachment. Common fields are typed; additional fields are captured via flatten.

color.description: Attachment sidebar color (hex string, e.g., "#36a64f" for green).

fallback.description: Plain-text summary shown in notifications that cannot render attachments.

footer.description: Small text shown at the bottom of the attachment.

pretext.description: Text shown above the attachment block.

text.description: Main body text of the attachment (supports template variables).

title.description: Bold title text at the top of the attachment.

SlackBlock.description: A Slack Block Kit block element. Common fields are typed; additional block-type-specific fields are captured via flatten.

text.description: Text object for the block (used by header, section, context types).

emoji.description: Whether to render emoji shortcodes (plain_text only).

after.description: Services that must start before this app.

args.description: Additional arguments passed to the command.

before.description: Services that must start after this app.

command.description: Command to run (relative to snap root).

command_chain.description: Wrapper commands run before the main command.

completer.description: Path to bash completion script relative to snap.

daemon.description: Daemon type: simple, forking, oneshot, notify, dbus.

desktop.description: Path to .desktop file relative to snap.

environment.description: Environment variables for the app (supports string, integer, and boolean values).

passthrough.description: Arbitrary YAML passed through to snap.yaml.

post_stop_command.description: Command to run after daemon stops.

restart_condition.description: Restart condition: on-failure, always, on-success, on-abnormal, on-abort, on-watchdog, never.

stop_command.description: Command to gracefully stop the daemon.

stop_mode.description: How to stop the daemon: sigterm, sigkill, etc.

apps.description: Application configurations defining daemons, commands, env vars.

base.description: Runtime base snap: core, core18, core20, core22, core24, bare.

channel_templates.description: Distribution channels: edge, beta, candidate, stable.

confinement.description: Security confinement level: strict, devmode, classic.

extra_files.description: Additional static files to bundle (string shorthand or structured form).

hooks.description: Snap hooks — maps hook name to arbitrary hook config.

id.description: Unique identifier for this snapcraft config.

ids.description: Build IDs to include. Empty means all builds.

name.description: Snap package name in the store.

name_template.description: Template for the output snap filename.

plugs.description: Top-level snap plug definitions (structured map). Keys are plug names, values are either `null` (simple plug) or an object with `interface` and optional attributes (e.g. `{ interface: "content", target: "$SNAP/shared" }`). GoReleaser uses `map[string]any` for this field.

publish.description: Whether to publish to the snapcraft store.

replace.description: Remove source archives from artifacts, keeping only snap.

skip.description: Skip this snapcraft config. Accepts bool or template string (e.g. `"{{ if .IsSnapshot }}true{{ endif }}"` for conditional skip). Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat with imported GoReleaser configs (GR's snapcraft config field is `pkg/config/config.go:1033` `Disable string`).

templated_extra_files.description: Extra files whose contents are rendered through the template engine before bundling. Unlike `extra_files` which copy as-is, template variables like `{{ .Tag }}` are expanded. GoReleaser Pro feature.

SnapcraftExtraFileSpec.description: Specifies an extra file for snapcraft. Can be a simple source path string or a structured object with source, destination, and mode fields (matching GoReleaser's SnapcraftExtraFiles).

anyOf.description: Structured form with source, destination, and mode.

bind.description: Bind-mount a directory to the snap's layout.

bind_file.description: Bind-mount a single file to the snap's layout.

symlink.description: Symlink a path to a location in the snap.

version_template.description: Version string template for snapshot builds (e.g., "{{ .Commit }}-SNAPSHOT"). F3: accepts the deprecated `name_template:` GR alias (renamed to `version_template` upstream). GR ref: `internal/pipe/snapshot/snapshot.go:25-28` — `if NameTemplate != "" { VersionTemplate = NameTemplate }`. A deprecation warning is emitted at config-load time when the alias is hit (see `apply_snapshot_legacy_aliases`).

enabled.description: When true, generate a source code archive for the release.

files.description: Extra files to include in the source archive. Accepts strings (glob patterns) or objects with src/dst/info.

format.description: Archive format for the source tarball: tar.gz, tgz, tar, or zip (default: tar.gz).

name_template.description: Filename template for the source archive (supports templates).

prefix_template.description: Prefix prepended to all paths inside the archive (supports templates). Defaults to name_template value. Use this to set a different prefix than the archive name.

SourceFileEntry.description: An individual file entry for the source archive, supporting src/dst mapping and file metadata overrides.

dst.description: Destination path within the archive prefix directory.

src.description: Source file path or glob pattern.

strip_parent.description: Strip the parent directory from the source path.

SourceFileInfo.description: File metadata overrides for source archive entries.

mode.description: File permissions mode. Accepts a YAML int (decimal) or an octal-prefixed string (`"0o755"`, `"0755"`). Stored as a `u32` after parsing — see [`StringOrU32`].

mtime.description: Modification time in RFC3339 format (supports templates).

bins.description: Build IDs whose binaries are bundled into the source RPM. When set, only artifacts produced by builds with these IDs are packaged. Mirrors GR `NFPM.Builds`.

build_host.description: Override the build host recorded in the RPM header. Useful for reproducible builds where the actual hostname leaks build-env detail.

contents.description: Additional contents to include in the source RPM. Shares the unified [`NfpmContent`] type with nFPM contents; SRPM-style `source:` / `destination:` / `type:` keys are accepted via serde aliases.

enabled.description: Enable source RPM generation. Default: false.

import_path.description: Project import path (Go-style; for Rust this is the canonical repository URL, e.g. `github.com/owner/repo`). Used in spec file generation for downstream tooling that expects a vcs-rooted path.

posttrans.description: `%posttrans` scriptlet — executed *after* all packages in the transaction have been installed. Path to a script file.

prefixes.description: Filesystem prefixes the package may install to (RPM `Prefix:` tag). Each entry becomes one `Prefix:` directive — relocatable RPMs need at least one prefix declared.

prerelease.description: Prerelease suffix appended to the version (e.g. `rc1`, `beta2`). Mirrors GR `NFPM.Prerelease`.

pretrans.description: `%pretrans` scriptlet — executed on the package transaction *before* any package in the transaction is installed. Path to a script file.

signature.description: RPM signature configuration. Shares the unified [`NfpmSignatureConfig`] type with nFPM.

skip.description: Skip this config. Accepts bool or template string.

spec_file.description: Path to the RPM spec file template.

version_metadata.description: Build metadata appended to the version (e.g. git commit hash). Mirrors GR `NFPM.VersionMetadata`.

StringOrBool.description: A value that can be either a bool or a template string. Used by `skip`, `skip_upload`, and similar fields across multiple config structs to support both `skip: true` and template conditionals like `skip: "{{ if .IsSnapshot }}true{{ endif }}"`.

cmd.description: Command to run (passed through the shell).

dir.description: Working directory for the command (defaults to project root).

output.description: When true, capture and log stdout/stderr of the command.

branch_history.description: Branch history mode for determining the previous tag: "full" or "last".

custom_tag.description: Custom version tag to use instead of auto-incrementing.

default_bump.description: Default version bump type when no conventional commit token is found: "major", "minor", "patch", or "none".

force_without_changes.description: When true, create a new tag even if no commits have changed since the last tag.

force_without_changes_pre.description: Like force_without_changes but only for pre-release versions.

git_api_tagging.description: When true, use the GitHub/GitLab API for tagging instead of git CLI.

initial_version.description: Version string to use when no previous tag exists (default: "0.1.0").

major_string_token.description: Conventional commit token triggering a major bump (default: "major").

minor_string_token.description: Conventional commit token triggering a minor bump (default: "minor" or "feat").

none_string_token.description: Conventional commit token suppressing a version bump entirely (default: "none").

patch_string_token.description: Conventional commit token triggering a patch bump (default: "patch" or "fix").

prerelease.description: When true, apply a pre-release suffix to the generated version.

release_branches.description: Branch name patterns (supports wildcards) that trigger releases (default: ["master", "main"]).

tag_context.description: Source for determining the previous tag: "repo" (default) or "branch".

tag_post_hooks.description: Commands to run after `anodizer tag` successfully creates and pushes the tag. Env and template vars same as `tag_pre_hooks`.

tag_pre_hooks.description: Commands to run before `anodizer tag` creates the tag. Useful for updating lockfiles or committing sibling changes that must be part of the tagged commit. Env: `ANODIZER_CURRENT_TAG`, `ANODIZER_PREVIOUS_TAG` are set; template vars `{{ .Tag }}`, `{{ .PreviousTag }}`, `{{ .Version }}`, `{{ .PrefixedTag }}` are available.

tag_prefix.description: Prefix prepended to version tags (e.g., "v" produces "v1.2.3").

verbose.description: When true, print verbose tag calculation output.

color.description: Theme color for the card (hex string, e.g. "0076D7").

enabled.description: Enable Microsoft Teams announcements (supports template expressions).

icon_url.description: Icon URL displayed in the card header.

message_template.description: Message template for the Adaptive Card body. Default: "{{ .ProjectName }} {{ .Tag }} is out! Check it out at {{ .ReleaseURL }}"

title_template.description: Title template for the Adaptive Card header. Default: "{{ ProjectName }} {{ Tag }} is out!"

bot_token.description: Telegram Bot API token. Get one from @BotFather.

chat_id.description: Telegram chat ID to send the message to (supports template variables).

enabled.description: Enable Telegram announcements. Requires bot_token and chat_id (supports template expressions).

message_template.description: Message template. Default: "{{ .ProjectName }} {{ .Tag }} is out! Check it out at {{ .ReleaseURL }}"

message_thread_id.description: Message thread ID for sending to a specific topic in a forum group. Supports template expressions; parsed to i64 at runtime.

TemplateFileConfig.description:
Configuration for a template file that is rendered through the template engine and placed in the dist directory as a release artifact.

GoReleaser Pro feature: all rendered template files are uploaded to the release by default. Both `src` and `dst` paths support template rendering.

dst.description: Destination filename, prefixed with the dist directory. Templates: allowed.

id.description: Identifier for this template file entry (default: "default").

mode.description: File permissions in octal notation as a string, e.g. `"0755"` (default: `"0655"`). Parsed at runtime via `parse_octal_mode()` to avoid YAML interpreting as decimal.

src.description: Source template file path. The file contents are rendered through the template engine. Templates: allowed (in path itself).

TemplatedExtraFile.description: A file whose contents are rendered through the template engine before use. Used by `templated_extra_files` across multiple stages (GoReleaser Pro feature).

dst.description: Destination filename for the rendered output. Supports template variables (e.g. `"{{ .ProjectName }}-NOTES.txt"`).

mode.description: File permissions in octal notation as a string, e.g. `"0755"`. Parsed at runtime via `parse_octal_mode()` to avoid YAML interpreting as decimal.

enabled.description: Enable Twitter/X announcements. Requires TWITTER_CONSUMER_KEY, TWITTER_CONSUMER_SECRET, TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN, TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET env vars (supports template expressions).

message_template.description: Tweet message template. Default: "{{ .ProjectName }} {{ .Tag }} is out! Check it out at {{ .ReleaseURL }}"

hooks.description: Pre/post hooks around universal binary creation.

id.description: Unique identifier for this universal binary, propagated into the artifact's metadata as `id` (GoReleaser universalbinary.go:42-44).

ids.description: Build IDs filter: only combine artifacts from builds whose `id` is in this list.

mod_timestamp.description: Override the modification timestamp for reproducible universal binaries.

name_template.description: Output filename template for the universal binary (supports templates).

replace.description: When true, remove the individual arch binaries after creating the universal binary.

checksum_header.description: Header name for the SHA256 checksum of the artifact.

client_x509_cert.description: Path to PEM-encoded client X.509 certificate for mTLS.

client_x509_key.description: Path to PEM-encoded client X.509 key for mTLS.

custom_artifact_name.description: When true, use the artifact name as-is (don't append to target URL).

custom_headers.description: Custom HTTP headers (each value is template-expanded).

extra_files.description: Extra files to include in uploading.

extra_files_only.description: Upload only extra files, skip normal artifacts.

exts.description: File extension filter: only upload artifacts with these extensions.

ids.description: Build IDs filter: only upload artifacts whose `id` is in this list.

meta.description: Include metadata artifacts in uploaded artifacts.

method.description: HTTP method: PUT or POST (default: PUT).

name.description: Human-readable name for this upload config.

password.description: Password for HTTP basic auth (env var template strongly recommended; in-config plaintext leaves the value in `dist/config.yaml` after dry-run). Resolution order: rendered `password` template → env `UPLOAD_{NAME}_SECRET`. Mirrors GoReleaser's `Upload.Password` cascade (added in upstream v2.12).

skip.description: Skip condition template (if rendered to "true", skip this upload).

target.description: Target URL template (supports template variables like {{ .ProjectName }}, {{ .Version }}).

trusted_certificates.description: Path to PEM-encoded trusted CA certificates.

username.description: Username for HTTP basic auth. Resolution order: rendered `username` template → env `UPLOAD_{NAME}_USERNAME`. Set this to a literal value or a `{{ .Env.X }}` template.

brute.description: Use brute-force compression (--brute flag). Very slow but produces smallest output.

compress.description: UPX compression level string (e.g., "1"-"9", "best"). Maps to `--compress` flag.

enabled.description: Whether to compress binaries with UPX. Accepts bool or template string (GoReleaser parity: `tmpl.Bool(upx.Enabled)`).

id.description: Unique identifier for this UPX config.

ids.description: Build IDs filter: only compress binaries from builds whose `id` is in this list.

required.description: When true, fail the build if UPX is not found.

targets.description: Target triples to compress binaries for (empty means all targets).

enabled.description: When true, synchronize the crate version with the git tag during release.

mode.description: Sync mode: "cargo" (updates Cargo.toml) or "tag" (derives version from tag).

enabled.description: Enable generic webhook announcements (supports template expressions).

expected_status_codes.description: HTTP status codes to accept as success (default: [200, 201, 202, 204]).

headers.description:
Custom HTTP headers to include in the request.

Precedence — **anodizer diverges from GoReleaser here**: - anodizer: a config-supplied `Authorization` header wins over the `BASIC_AUTH_HEADER_VALUE` / `BEARER_TOKEN_HEADER_VALUE` env var. - GoReleaser (webhook.go:104-115): env-supplied `Authorization` is appended FIRST; most servers honour the first occurrence, so the env value effectively wins.

Migrating configs that relied on env-overriding the config header must either remove the config entry or be reconfigured. Use templated config (`Authorization: "Bearer {{ .Env.MY_TOKEN }}"`) for the cleanest migration.

message_template.description: Message body template. Default: "{{ .ProjectName }} {{ .Tag }} is out! Check it out at {{ .ReleaseURL }}"

skip_tls_verify.description: When true, skip TLS certificate verification for the webhook endpoint.

amd64_variant.description: amd64 microarchitecture variant filter (e.g. "v1", "v2", "v3", "v4"). Only artifacts matching this variant are included. Default: "v1".

description.description: Full package description displayed in the WinGet gallery.

ids.description: Build IDs filter: only include artifacts whose `id` is in this list.

installation_notes.description: Post-install notes shown to the user.

name.description: Override the package name (default: crate name).

package_identifier.description: WinGet package identifier (e.g. "Publisher.AppName"). Auto-generated if empty.

package_name.description: Package name as displayed (default: same as name).

path.description: Manifest file path (auto-generated if empty from publisher/name/version).

product_code.description: Product code for the installer (used in Add/Remove Programs).

publisher_url.description: Publisher homepage URL shown in the WinGet manifest.

repository.description: Unified repository config with branch, token, PR, git SSH support. (Replaces the legacy `manifests_repo: WingetManifestsRepoConfig`.)

skip_upload.description: Skip publishing. `"true"` always skips; `"auto"` skips for prereleases. Accepts bool or template string.

url_template.description: Custom URL template for download URLs (overrides release URL).

minimum_version.description: Minimum required version of the dependency.

package_identifier.description: WinGet package identifier of the dependency (e.g., "Publisher.App").

WorkspaceConfig.description: A workspace represents an independent project root within a monorepo. Each workspace has its own crates, changelog, and release configuration, allowing independently-versioned components that aren't Cargo workspace members.

after.description: Hooks run after this workspace's pipeline completes.

before.description: Hooks run before this workspace's pipeline starts.

binary_signs.description: Binary-specific signing configs (same shape as `signs` but only for binary artifacts). The `artifacts` field on each entry is constrained at parse time to `binary` / `none` (or omitted) — a broader filter on `binary_signs` would silently match nothing because the loop only iterates Binary artifacts. Constraint lives in `deserialize_binary_signs`.

env.description:
Environment variables scoped to this workspace.

List of `KEY=VALUE` strings (GoReleaser parity). Order is preserved. Values are template-rendered at pipeline startup.

name.description: Workspace identifier used in logs and template variables.

signs.description: Signing configurations for binaries, archives, and checksums.

skip.description: Pipeline stages to skip when releasing this workspace. Stage names match the CLI `--skip` flag (e.g., `announce`, `publish`).
